description: This is a cross-sectional micrograph showing discrete small oxide nodules formed on Fe–10Mn–5Al alloy after isothermal oxidation for 24 h at 800°C under 200 torr pure oxygen, with a 7 μm scale bar provided for size reference. The lower bright region corresponds to the Fe-Mn-Al alloy matrix, the upper dark region is the sample mounting medium, and the irregular raised features at the interface are the oxide nodules. As reported in the supporting text and caption, these small nodules (formed in alloys with Mn content >7.5 wt%) consist of an outer grey manganese oxide layer at the gas-oxide interface, an underlying white Fe₂O₃ layer, dispersed Al-rich phase particles inside the oxide, and a continuous Al-rich layer at the nodule-alloy interface. This figure validates that Mn content above ~7.5 wt% leads to formation of Mn-rich small oxide nodules instead of Fe-rich large nodules, supporting the compositional dependence of oxide scale morphology in high-temperature oxidized Fe-Mn-Al alloy systems.
